title: Doc sweep: the two grills' descriptions stop implying a shared skill
description: After EXEC-083, sweep the persistent docs that describe the Setup Grill as running execution-grill-with-docs: the root Instance's .excn/CONTEXT.md (Setup Skill and Setup Grill / Work Grill glossary entries), .excn/PROCESS.md 'Two grills, kept separate' and their src/template seeds where the wording appears. Each grill's docs name only its own skill(s): the Setup Grill is the to-execution skill end-to-end; the Work Grill is execution-grill-with-docs → execution-to-prd → execution-to-issues. Src-First where a swept file has a template counterpart.
acceptance_criteria: No persistent doc states or implies the Setup Grill runs execution-grill-with-docs
CONTEXT.md glossary entries (Setup Skill, Setup Grill / Work Grill) match the post-EXEC-083 reality
Template seeds and root copies agree (Src-First)
Chartered-change gates pass (alignment on persistent-doc edits)
fix: Three surgical edits to .excn/CONTEXT.md: Setup Skill entry's Preflight parenthetical corrected (Node 18+, no global skills — Scaffolder stamps setup's needs and the later Work Grill's); the 'reads lightly, like grill-with-docs: a thin orchestrator' analogy replaced (carries its own Setup Grill interview, Grill phase runs from the skill file, no stamped skill invoked); Setup Grill / Work Grill entry states the Setup Grill runs inside the to-execution skill end-to-end. Work Grill sentence untouched. PROCESS.md and template seeds verified already correct — no other persistent doc implies the Setup Grill runs execution-grill-with-docs; root CONTEXT.md is variant with no template counterpart (Src-First imposes no mirror).
notes: Split from EXEC-083 at breakdown review (Brooks, 2026-06-12).
